_description: SEC NT 10-K / NT 10-Q Late Filing Notice scorer. Always negative. Severity depends on stated reason.
role: analyst reading an NT (notification of late filing) form
context: NT filings are short (usually 1-2 pages). The company states WHY it cannot file on time. The reason is the primary scoring signal.

system_or_administrative.label: SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE
system_or_administrative.note: Benign — technical or administrative delay with no fundamental concern

complex_transaction_or_acquisition.label: SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE to MILDLY_NEGATIVE
complex_transaction_or_acquisition.note: Deal complexity can justify delay but introduces uncertainty

revenue_recognition_review.label: MILDLY_NEGATIVE
revenue_recognition_review.note: Accounting issue — potential restatement risk, elevated concern

restatement_needed.label: NEGATIVE to VERY_NEGATIVE
restatement_needed.note: Material accounting error requiring restatement — significant red flag

auditor_change_during_filing.label: MILDLY_NEGATIVE
auditor_change_during_filing.note: Mid-cycle auditor change creates process disruption and raises questions

no_reason_stated.label: MILDLY_NEGATIVE
no_reason_stated.note: Unknown reason is treated as worse — lack of transparency is itself a signal

going_concern_investigation.label: VERY_NEGATIVE
going_concern_investigation.note: Existential risk signal — company may not be a going concern

mandatory_floor.label: SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE
mandatory_floor.note: NT filings are NEVER neutral or positive. Minimum penalty is -0.050 regardless of reason.

escalation_rule.description: If this is the Nth NT filing in the past 24 months, apply an additional penalty per prior NT.
escalation_rule.data_source: Check master_summary for prior NT filing dates
escalation_rule.note: Recurring inability to file on time is a strong negative pattern signal

tasks: Identify the NT type: NT 10-K (annual) or NT 10-Q (quarterly)
Extract the stated reason for the late filing from the form text
Classify the reason using reason_severity_map — pick the closest matching category
Apply escalation_rule: count prior NTs in master_summary within 24 months and add -0.100 per prior NT
Compute final score within the mapped range, respecting mandatory_floor of -0.050
Write a 1-sentence summary describing the NT type and classified reason
Write a short forecast (1-3 sentences) on likely next steps and risk outlook
Update master_summary with this NT filing date, type, and classified reason

output_schema.score: float in [-0.900, -0.050] — never positive or zero
output_schema.label: string — sentiment label matching score tier
output_schema.summary: string — 1-sentence description of the filing and reason
output_schema.forecast: string — 1-3 sentence outlook on risk and next steps
output_schema.prior_nt_count_24m: integer — number of prior NTs found in master_summary within 24 months
output_schema.escalation_penalty_applied: float — total additional penalty from escalation rule (0.0 if none)
output_schema.master_summary_update: object — fields to append/update in master_summary

rules: mandatory_floor: final score must never exceed -0.050 (never neutral, never positive)
never positive: NT filings represent failure to meet regulatory deadline — no upside scenario
pattern escalation: apply -0.100 per prior NT filing within 24 months on top of base score
if no reason is stated in the filing, default to 'no_reason_stated' category
when reason could fit multiple categories, choose the more severe classification
always update master_summary with this filing's date and classification for future escalation checks
